title: Constitution of India
section_title: Protection of life and personal liberty
text: Article 21 provides that no person shall be deprived of his life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. This record supports questions about personal liberty, life, lawful procedure, arrest, detention, and constitutional protection.

title: Constitution of India
section_title: Persons not to be deprived of property save by authority of law
text: Article 300A provides that no person shall be deprived of his property save by authority of law. This record supports questions about taking property, land possession, acquisition, compensation, and whether a public authority needs legal authority before depriving a person of property.

title: Constitution of India
section_title: Dismissal, removal or reduction in rank of persons employed in civil capacities
text: Article 311 protects civil servants against dismissal, removal, or reduction in rank without the safeguards required by law. It includes the principle that a person should not be dismissed, removed, or reduced in rank without being informed of the charges and given a reasonable opportunity of being heard, subject to constitutional exceptions.

title: Right to Information Act, 2005
text: Section 7 of the Right to Information Act, 2005 deals with disposal of RTI requests. The Public Information Officer must ordinarily provide the requested information or reject the request as expeditiously as possible, and in any case within thirty days of receiving the request. Where the information concerns life or liberty, it must be provided within forty-eight hours.

title: Right to Information Act, 2005
text: Section 19 of the Right to Information Act, 2005 provides the appeal mechanism. If an applicant does not receive a decision within the time specified in Section 7, or is aggrieved by a decision of the Public Information Officer, the applicant may file a first appeal within thirty days. A second appeal may be made to the Information Commission within ninety days, subject to condonation of delay where sufficient cause is shown.

title: Right to Information Act, 2005
section_title: Exemption from disclosure of personal information
text: Section 8(1)(j) of the Right to Information Act, 2005 concerns exemption from disclosure for information which relates to personal information. This record supports questions about whether a public authority may refuse disclosure on the ground that the requested material is personal information.

title: Consumer Protection Act, 2019
text: Section 39 of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019 empowers the District Commission, where defect, deficiency, unfair trade practice, or product liability is proved, to direct appropriate relief. Relief may include removal of defects, replacement of goods, return of price or charges paid, compensation for loss or injury, discontinuance of unfair trade practice, withdrawal of hazardous goods, corrective advertisement, and costs.

title: CCS (CCA) Rules
text: Rule 14 of the CCS (CCA) Rules concerns procedure for imposing major penalties in disciplinary matters. It is relevant where a government employee challenges a major penalty on grounds such as lack of charge memorandum, absence of inquiry, denial of reasonable opportunity, or breach of procedural fairness.
